flags.concern: The plan is built around a three-tier allocator, but the repository's current test contract already expects a different coexistence algorithm. Step 4 says the existing shrink-to-fit test should still pass, yet the checked-in test file expects `300/300` for the default 600px case, `200/400` when generations is 400px, and `156/94` in a 250px viewport. Current `getEffectivePaneHeights()` only implements the visibility short-circuit, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split, so removing the visibility gate without first resolving whether the new tests are authoritative will likely codify the wrong behavior.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:87-126` implements only the visibility gate, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split. `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx:221-289` expects equal split / preserve-generations / degraded-proportional outcomes that do not match that code.

flags.concern: Step 4 understates which existing assertions change if heights are always precomputed. The provider exposes `effective*` heights even when both panes are hidden, so the initial-state test will also fail after this change; the plan only updates the solo-editor test and says the rest are unchanged. Following the plan as written would leave `PanesContext.test.tsx` inconsistent with the new behavior.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx:139-153` currently asserts initial hidden-state values of `effectiveGensHeight=300` and `effectiveEditorHeight=540`. With the visibility gate removed in `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:102-106`, those exported values will be pre-constrained even before either pane is opened.
